public int[] transformArray(int[] nums) {
    int zeroCount = 0;
    for (int num : nums) {
        zeroCount += (num % 2 == 0) ? 1 : 0;
    }
    int[] res = new int[nums.length];
    for (int i = 0; i < zeroCount; i++) {
        res[i] = 0;
    }
    for (int i = zeroCount; i < nums.length; i++) {
        res[i] = 1;
    }
    return res;
}

        res[i] = 0;
    }
    for (int i = zeroCount; i < nums.length; i++) {
        res[i] = 1;
    }
    return res;
}

solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int[] transformArray(int[] nums) {
        int zeroCount = 0;
        for (int num : nums) {
            zeroCount += num % 2 == 0 ? 1 : 0;
        }
        int[] res = new int[nums.length];
        for (int i = 0; i < zeroCount; i++) {
            res[i] = 0;
        }
        for (int i = zeroCount; i < nums.length; i++) {
            res[i] = 1;
        }
        return res;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Count even numbers (0s) and construct the result array with all 0s followed by 1s. Avoids sorting by leveraging binary nature of transformed values. Strengths: Linear time. Weakness: Only applicable for 0/1 binary transformation.
